title: Leerpad 4B · De Trein
subtitle: Train timetables — vertrek, aankomst, spoor, prijs

questions.q: How do you ask 'At what time does the train to Brussels depart?'
options: Wanneer is de trein in Brussel?
Hoe laat vertrekt de trein naar Brussel?
Hoe laat komt de trein in Brussel aan?
Op welk spoor is de trein naar Brussel?
questions.explanation: 'Hoe laat vertrekt...' = at what time does ... depart. The verb 'vertrekken' means to depart. 'naar Brussel' = to Brussels.

questions.q: What does 'De trein komt om 12:15 in Brussel aan' mean?
options: The train departs from Brussels at 12:15
The train arrives in Brussels at 12:15
The train stops in Brussels at 12:15
questions.explanation: 'aankomen' = to arrive. It's a SEPARABLE verb — 'aan' goes to the END of the main clause: 'De trein KOMT om 12:15 in Brussel AAN.'

questions.q: Which preposition fits: '___ welk spoor vertrekt de trein?'
questions.explanation: 'Op welk spoor' = on which platform. Spoor is treated as a place (a flat surface), so OP, not OM. OM is only for clock times.

questions.q: How would you write '11:45' as it is SPOKEN?
questions.explanation: Written: '11:45 uur'. Spoken: 'elf uur vijfenveertig'. Belgian Dutch standardly includes 'uur' in spoken time. Alternative: 'kwart voor twaalf' (quarter to twelve).

questions.q: How do you say 'a return ticket'?
questions.explanation: 'Retourticket' = return ticket. 'Enkele reis' = one-way. The cashier may ask: 'Enkele reis of retour?'
Singular subject ('een ticket'), so the verb 'kosten' takes the 3rd-person singular form: kost. Plural would be 'twee tickets KOSTEN vijftig euro'.

questions.q: Where does 'aan' go in: 'De trein komt om half drie in Dinant ___'?
options: Aan stays attached: 'aankomt' at the start
At the very end: 'De trein komt om half drie in Dinant aan'
Right after the verb: 'komt aan om half drie'
questions.explanation: Separable verbs split in main clauses — the prefix goes to the END. 'aankomen' → 'De trein KOMT ... AAN'. Same pattern as 'opstaan', 'meegaan', 'uitgaan'.
Bestemming = destination. Other key words: dienstregeling (timetable), vertrek (departure), aankomst (arrival), spoor (platform).
